title: Quantitative bounds: $\|\beta_1\|_\infty\lesssim R_1S_1^{-2}$, $\|F^*\omega_i\|\lesssim S_1^{-2}$, and $\Dil_2(g\circ f)\lesssim1$

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Several quantitative estimates are used and should be recorded with full justification:

1. $\Dil_1(g)\lesssim1\Rightarrow\Dil_2(g)\lesssim1$ and $\Dil_2(g\circ f)\le\Dil_2(g)\Dil_2(f)\lesssim1$ (sub-multiplicativity of $k$-dilation under composition; $\Dil_2\le\Dil_1^2$ pointwise on $\Lambda^2$).
2. $\|F^*\omega_i\|_{L^\infty}\le\Dil_2(F)\,\|\omega_i\|_{L^\infty}\lesssim S_1^{-2}$, i.e. the pointwise bound relating the comass of a pulled-back 2-form to the 2-dilation.
3. The Poincare-lemma primitive $\beta_1=\sum_j(\int_0^{x_1}c_j\,dx_1')dx_j$ satisfies $d\beta_1=F^*\omega_1$ (closedness needed) and $\|\beta_1\|_\infty\lesssim R_1\|F^*\omega_1\|_\infty\lesssim R_1S_1^{-2}$, using $0\le x_1\le R_1$.

These are standard but load-bearing (they produce the key inequality $R_1^{-1}S_1^2S_2S_3\lesssim\Vol_3(z_3)$). A complete proof should state the comass/dilation comparison and the sub-multiplicativity precisely rather than asserting them (F3, minor).
